findings.evidence: `ComplexModule.lean:118` has the general compatible complex-module context, while `Finrank.lean:67` proves the formula directly from `Module.finrank_mul_finrank`; Mathlib also exposes the complex specialization as `finrank_real_of_complex` at `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Complex/FiniteDimensional.lean:57`.
findings.fix: First add a reusable theorem for `[AddCommGroup V] [Module ℝ V] [Module ℂ V] [IsScalarTower ℝ ℂ V]`, e.g. `Module.finrank ℝ V = 2 * Module.finrank ℂ V`, then derive the `J`-specific statement by installing `J.complexModule` and `J.complexModule_isScalarTower`.
findings.issue: `finrank_real_eq_two_mul_complexFinrank` specializes the tower-law dimension formula to `AlmostComplexStructure`, even though the nearby API already handles arbitrary `[Module ℂ V] [IsScalarTower ℝ ℂ V]` via `ofComplexModule`.

summary: The finrank result is proved only as an almost-complex special case, but the natural API level is any compatible complex module structure; the almost-complex theorem should be a corollary.
